Home / Paintings / Spring
Back to Gallery
Exhibition Room

Spring

The garden's first awakening — anemones, hellebores and early ferns emerging beneath bare spring branches.

Wood Anemones

Anemone nemorosa carpets the forest floor each April, its star-shaped leaves opening before the white blossoms unfurl. These two works follow the same drift of flowers through shifting light, from cool jewel tones to a warm golden dusk.

No. 1-1
Wood Anemones I
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas
No. 1-2
Wood Anemones II
Nona Dronova · 2023 · Oil on canvas

Hyacinths, Forget-Me-Nots and Primrose

Blue hyacinth spires rise above a low tide of forget-me-nots and primrose, painted with loose, gestural strokes. The palette stays true to a spring border at its most crowded and fragrant.

No. 1-3
Hyacinths, Forget-Me-Nots and Primrose
Nona Dronova · 2020 · Oil on canvas

Daffodils and Crocuses, Italy

Painted during a spring in Italy, this study gathers narcissus and purple crocus into a single joyful clearing, the white and violet playing off one another in the warm Mediterranean light.

No. 1-4
Daffodils and Crocuses, Italy
Nona Dronova · 2018 · Oil on canvas

Lily of the Valley

Convallaria majalis, with its arching leaves and beaded white bells, is one of the most quietly beloved flowers of the woodland spring. These studies move from a wide view of the colony to close, intimate details of the blossoms among the leaves.

No. 1-5
Lily of the Valley I
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas
No. 1-6
Lily of the Valley II
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas
No. 1-7
Lily of the Valley and Wildflowers
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas

Daffodils

Two studies of narcissus in full bloom, painted with bold colour against blue and green grounds. The flowers are treated almost as portraits, each face turned toward the light.

No. 1-8
Daffodils I
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas
No. 1-9
Daffodils II
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas

Irises, Lilac and Forget-Me-Nots, Italy

Purple bearded iris rise above a haze of lilac and a carpet of forget-me-nots, painted softly to capture the misted quality of an Italian spring morning.

No. 1-10
Irises, Lilac and Forget-Me-Nots, Italy
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas

Primrose Meadow

An abstracted view of a primrose meadow, where drifts of yellow, violet and blue dissolve into pure colour above a foreground of broad green leaves and primrose blossoms.

No. 1-11
Primrose Meadow
Nona Dronova · 2024 · Oil on canvas

Primroses and Fritillaria

Clusters of pale primrose gather beneath the nodding purple bells of fritillaria, a pairing that marks the height of the woodland spring.

No. 1-12
Primroses and Fritillaria
Nona Dronova · 2020 · Oil on canvas

Rhododendrons in the Forest

Pink rhododendron blossoms open in dappled forest light, their clustered flowers and dark evergreen leaves a signature of the woodland garden in late spring.

No. 1-13
Rhododendrons in the Forest
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas

Fritillaria, Lily of the Valley and Rhododendron

A layered spring composition bringing together chequered fritillaria, arching lily of the valley, grape hyacinth and flowering rhododendron in a single view of the garden.

No. 1-14
Fritillaria, Lily of the Valley and Rhododendron
Nona Dronova · 2019 · Oil on canvas

Rhododendron Blossom

A close, luminous study of rhododendron blossoms in shades of pink and magenta, set among broad grey-green leaves and ornamental grasses.

No. 1-15
Rhododendron Blossom
Nona Dronova · 2023 · Oil on canvas

Crown Imperial Fritillaria and Grape Hyacinths

The crown imperial's nodding orange bells rise above a bed of grape hyacinths, a dramatic pairing of colour and form at the height of spring.

No. 1-16
Crown Imperial Fritillaria and Grape Hyacinths
Nona Dronova · 2020 · Oil on canvas

Fritillaria, Daffodils and Rhododendron

Chequered fritillaria, white lily-of-the-valley bells, narcissus and pink rhododendron come together in a single generous view of a spring garden at its peak.

No. 1-17
Fritillaria, Daffodils and Rhododendron
Nona Dronova · Oil on canvas

More works from this series will be added to the gallery in due course.

The Garden as Art

A digital archive of artistic heritage, dedicated to the philosophy of the garden, contemporary art, and the creation of a harmonious living space.
